rulings.Case Title: IN RE: BELVA WATSON, ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Respondent Belva Watsons Notice Of Demurrer And Demurrer To First Amended Petition To Recover Real And Personal Property Belonging To Decedent And For Declaratory Relief; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support
rulings.Rulings: TENTATIVE RULING Moving party, Belva Watson's Demurrer to First Amended Petition is sustained, in part, with leave to amend. 1) The First Amended Petition is vague on its face as to the identity of the intended respondent(s). Specifically, Petitioner, Dorothy White should identify which decedent(s) and/or decedent(s)' estate she is seeking relief from. Moreover, Petitioner should clarify whether Belva Watson is being sued as an individual. Amended petition must be filed with the court within 10 court days from the scheduled hearing date. 2) Claims against Leroy Watson and/or his trust estate: (a)Respondent is equitably estopped from asserting the statute of limitations as an affirmative defense to claims against Leroy Watson and/or his trust estate. The pleading allegations are sufficient to demonstrate that Respondent induced Petitioner into not filing a timely claim. (b)Petitioner's causes of action for Unjust Enrichment, Common Count for Money Damages for Services Rendered, and Quantum Meruit are barred by res judicata due to the final decision of the Labor Commission on Petitioner's wage claim. Petitioner is also collaterally estopped from re-litigating the issue of whether an employment relationship existed. (c)Petitioner has pled sufficient facts as to the causes of action for Financial Elder Abuse, Fraud and Misrepresentation, Intentional Infliction of Emotional Distress, and Negligent Infliction of Emotional Distress. (d)Petitioner has not pled sufficient facts as to the cause of action for Conspiracy to Commit Fraud. Petitioner failed to make allegations to show two or more persons agreed to a common plan or design to commit fraud. 3) Claims against Laverne Watson and/or her trust estate: Demurrer is sustained for claims against Laverne Watson and/or trust estate because the claims are barred by the one year statute of limitation pursuant to CCP §366.2. Petitioner failed to plead sufficient facts to demonstrate equitable estoppel. DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED PETITION TO RECOVER REAL AND PERSONAL PROPERTY BELONGING TO DECEDENT AND FOR DECLARATORY RELIEF is tentatively sustained, in part, in a consistent manner with the above findings. Petitioner is granted leave to amend the First Amended Petition. Amended petition must be filed with the court within 10 court days from the scheduled hearing date. Any party who wishes to argue the motion must notify the court (415-551-3702) and opposing counsel no later than 5:00 p.m. on August 28, 2013. If the tentative ruling is not argued, it will become the order of the court. Prevailing party to prepare the order.
